description:
Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition is a Real-Time Strategy game centered around the medieval
ages and the various battles, conquests, and wars of history.
game: Age Of Empires II: Definitive Edition

0.0.1.description:
This release is barely functional and can only run one Age2scenario file. There is no logic whatsoever at the moment. But for the curious/eager it is here.

Place the folders in the zip file into your local Age of Empires II DE steam folder. You can find the folder either by navigating to `C:/Users/<USER>/Games/Age of Empires 2 DE/<STRING_OF_NUMBERS>/` or by opening Age 2 and opening the scenario editor. From there, click "Open Scenario Folder" and navigate up in your file explorer to the folder that is a long string of numbers.

You will need to also select `C:/Users/<USER>/Games/Age of Empires 2 DE/<STRING_OF_NUMBERS>/` when first running the Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition Client. If you mess this up, it is OK, because there is a command to reselect it.

At the moment, the folder prompt will need to be selected every time you run the client, but the folder should be already selected in the prompt.

## New Contributors
* @Stefrex1 made their first contribution in https://github.com/dmwever/Ageipelago/pull/1

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/dmwever/Ageipelago/commits/0.0.1
0.0.1.title: Release 0.0.1 - The Proof-of-Concept Release (Unstable) (No Logic)

0.1.0.description:
This release adds the full playthrough of Attila the Hun campaign as a playable AP world.
- Shuffles many triggers into the AP world, such as unlocking Bleda's Camp.
- Shuffles starting resources.

Place the folders in the zip file into your local Age of Empires II DE steam folder. You can find the folder either by navigating to C:/Users/`<USER>`/Games/Age of Empires 2 DE/`<STEAM_ID>`/ or by opening Age 2 and opening the scenario editor. From there, click "Open Scenario Folder" and navigate up in your file explorer to the folder named after your steam id.

You will need to also select C:/Users/`<USER>`/Games/Age of Empires 2 DE/`<STEAM_ID>`/ when first running the Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition Client. If you mess this up, it is OK, because there is a command to reselect it.

If you are stuck or think you found a bug, first click [here](https://github.com/dmwever/Ageipelago/blob/main/docs/AP%20FAQ.md) to view the AP-specific FAQ.
0.1.0.title: Release 0.1.0 - Attila the Hun - Playable Alpha Release

0.1.1.description:
- Fix issue where the client won't connect to a campaign.
- Attila: The Scourge of God: Kill the Iron Boar trigger now properly detects the boar is killed.
- **NEW COMMAND:** /refresh: disconnects and reconnects to the campaign or scenario. Usually only needed when quickly switching between campaigns or standalone scenarios.
0.1.1.title: Release 0.1.1

- Roman Camps no longer in logic before starting base on Attila 1 and 2.
- Scythian Palisades removed after resolving Bleda storyline.

- Messages are now resent if messages.xsdat is missing.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/dmwever/Ageipelago/compare/0.1.1...0.1.2

0.1.4.title: Release 0.1.4

0.2.0.description:
## What's Changed

### New Campaign: Joan of Arc
- All 6 scenarios from the original base game.

### New Setting: Shuffle Buildings
- When enabled, buildings are shuffled into the pool. When disabled, buildings are given to the player as starting items.
- Buildings take into account what civilizations are being played. Attila the Hun will never expect the player to need houses, and Joan of Arc won't ask for Feitorias.
- 6 different options for shuffling:
 1. _Shuffle Economy:_ Economy buildings are shuffled and must be unlocked, such as TC, houses, market, and dropsites. Will shuffle docks.
 2. _Shuffle Tech:_ Tech buildings such as blacksmith, university, and monastery are shuffled.
 3. _Shuffle Military:_ Military buildings are shuffled, including dock and Castle
 4. _Shuffle Defense:_ Walls, towers, and castle are shuffled. Includes outpost.
 5. _Shuffle Unique:_ In the future, unique buildings will be shuffled, currently doesn't do anything except keep unique buildings out of your starting inventory.
 6. _Shuffle Wonder:_ The wonder the wonder the... no!

### New Feature: APavilion
- Each scenario now has a pavilion that allows them to declare victory once they beat a campaign. This will allow the player to obtain more checks even after winning a scenario.
- The pavilion will flash AP colors when the player has won the scenario and will have a tech called "Victory".
- Upon subsequent visits to a completed scenario, the player may declare victory at any point.
- In the future, mercenaries and one-time resources will be located here for the player's digression.

### Logic Overhaul
- Starting resources are now dynamically generated to fill space. Players can now expect to have at least 1000 of gold, food, and wood and 500 stone by completion of the apworld. If the item pool is too small for that amount of items, players will receive less starting resources.
- Building locations require the player to be able to reach their age and have villagers. Dynamically adjusts to unlocked scenarios.
- All Attila the Hun locations have much stricter logic (makes things easier).
- All opponents require a building with a counter to the units they build. Much thanks to @Stefrex1 for researching unit compositions for every scenario.

### Updated Setting: Enabled Campaigns
- Now accepts Joan of Arc as an option. When selecting multiple Campaigns, all campaigns must be finished to goal.

### Updated Setting: Starting Campaigns
- Now accepts Joan of Arc as an option. Each selected campaign will be given as a starting item.

### Updated Setting: Scenario Branching
- Scenario Branching All: affects Joan of Arc scenarios 2 and 3. In both campaigns, each individual castle is a check.
- Scenario Branching Any: Destroying any castle in Joan of Arc 2 gives a single check. Destroy any three castles in Joan of Arc 3 for one check each.

### QOL
- Scenario-Specific checks are now listed as objectives in each scenario. At the moment, there is no difference between Branching Any and Branching All for which checks are displayed.

### Client Changes
- The client will now only connects to the game when it is connected to the AP world server.
- The player is now notified when the game is connected to the client.

### Bugfixes
- World now actually goals 11
- Scenario items no longer bleed into other scenarios.
- Scenarios now wait for a connection before attempting to load startup data.
- Removed starting resources from existing scenarios.
- Failed reads and writes no longer display "Wrong AP World Version" errors.

### Known Issues
- In Joan of Arc 2, if one of the English players is defeated, the other surrenders as in vanilla, deleting their castles and giving free checks.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/dmwever/Ageipelago/compare/0.1.4...0.2.0
0.2.0.title: Release 0.2.0 - The Building Update

0.2.1.description:
## What's Changed
